summary: Partially decompiled the common fighter shouldered state by moving the 0x8009C4F8-0x8009CA08 region out of asm/ft_08A4.s into new ftCo_09C4.c and ftCo_Shouldered.c translation units. The PR report shows ftCo_09C4.c fully matched and ftCo_Shouldered.c at 90.48%, with trivial IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks matched and several core shouldered routines still using ASM/WIP fallback. The change also introduced supporting headers, added the new objects to obj_files.mk, renamed the interacted-fighter pointer to victim_gobj across many fighter/capture/throw paths, and refined relevant Fighter/common-data/collision structs to make the shouldered code expressible.
what_changed: Removed the shouldered-related assembly block from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s, including ftCo_8009C4F8, ftCo_8009C540, ftCo_8009C5A4, ftCo_8009C640, ftCo_8009C744, ftCo_Shouldered_Anim, ftCo_Shouldered_IASA, ftCo_Shouldered_Phys, and ftCo_Shouldered_Coll.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_09C4.c for ftCo_8009C4F8 and ftCo_8009C540; the PR report says this file matched 100%.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Shouldered.c with C/WIP implementations and inline-asm fallback for the main shouldered routines; the PR report says the file reached 90.48%.
Added ftCo_09C4.h and ftCo_Shouldered.h declarations and included ftCo_Shouldered.h from many ftCommon cargo/wait/attack/item files that call ftCo_8009C5A4 or related routines.
Updated obj_files.mk to build ftCo_09C4.c.o and ftCo_Shouldered.c.o before the remaining ft_08A4.s.o, preserving link/order placement while shrinking the asm object.
Renamed Fighter::x1A58_interactedFighter to Fighter::victim_gobj and propagated that name through common fighter, cargo, Koopa, Mewtwo, Master Hand, and other capture/throw call sites.
Added ftCommon_MotionVars::shouldered with fields at fp+2340 and fp+2344, matching the shouldered timer/flag storage used by ftCo_Shouldered_Anim and ftCo_8009C640.
Refined ftCommonData fields used by shouldered logic, including x3B0 as float, x3B4 as shouldered_anim_rate, and x4A0/x4A4/x4A8/x4AC as floats.
Refined several Fighter bitfields and flags from UnkFlagStruct-style access to direct named bitfields such as x2224_b2, x2224_b7, x221F_b4, throw_flags, and related byte/bit names.
Adjusted HitCapsule naming/layout in lbcollision.h, including exposing damage and kb_angle fields that the shouldered damage code reads.
smart_moves: Split the fully matched prologue/helper pair ftCo_8009C4F8 and ftCo_8009C540 into ftCo_09C4.c while keeping the harder shouldered state code in its own ftCo_Shouldered.c; this makes progress searchable and isolates partial-match debt.
Used the existing project pattern of ASM functions with non-MUST_MATCH/WIP C bodies and nofralloc inline-asm fallback for routines that were close but not fully matching.
Kept empty callbacks as normal C functions for ftCo_Shouldered_IASA, ftCo_Shouldered_Phys, and ftCo_Shouldered_Coll, immediately removing trivial assembly and improving action-state readability.

Renamed the ubiquitous x1A58_interactedFighter field to victim_gobj before/while matching the shouldered code, making the chain of holder/victim access in cargo, capture, and damage code much clearer.
Introduced typed common-data floats and a named shouldered_anim_rate field so animation-rate and timer code could be written semantically instead of through opaque UNK_T accesses.

Added new object files before the remaining ft_08A4.s.o rather than deleting the asm object entirely, allowing incremental decompilation of a contiguous assembly file without disturbing later still-asm symbols.
decomp_lessons: For partial matches, separating a fully matched small file from a larger partially matched state file gives future contributors a clean boundary and avoids burying matched routines inside WIP code.
When a decompiled action state depends on a heavily reused fighter pointer, renaming the struct field globally can be a prerequisite for readable and maintainable C.
State-specific motion variables should be added to the ftCommon_MotionVars union at the observed offsets before trying to express action logic; here fp+2340/fp+2344 became mv.co.shouldered.x0/x4.
Common-data fields should be typed to their actual use once a routine proves they are floats or rates; this avoids casts and helps match arithmetic and ftAnim_SetAnimRate calls.
Inline ASM fallback can keep the repository buildable and symbol-order-correct while C versions document current understanding under WIP/non-MUST_MATCH guards.

Damage/collision matching often requires improving HitCapsule field names first; ftCo_Shouldered.c reads hit->damage, hit->kb_angle, hit->x8, and hit->element-like data rather than opaque vector aliases.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action files follow ftCo_<Action>.c and ftCo_<Action>.h, as introduced with ftCo_Shouldered.c/h.
Small address-range helper files may keep an address-style name such as ftCo_09C4.c/h when the behavior name is not yet fully known.
Unidentified functions retain address names such as ftCo_8009C5A4, ftCo_8009C640, and ftCo_8009C744, while action callbacks use semantic names such as ftCo_Shouldered_Anim.
The field x1A58_interactedFighter was renamed to victim_gobj, indicating that grabbed/captured/shouldered relationships should prefer role-based names when evidence is strong.
Action-local motion variables use a state substruct under mv.co, here mv.co.shouldered.x0 and mv.co.shouldered.x4, with offset comments preserving fp+2340/fp+2344.
Common-data semantic naming was introduced where known, notably shouldered_anim_rate at +3B4, while less-understood fields remain offset-style such as x3B0 and x4A8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used ASM function declarations with C bodies enabled for !MUST_MATCH or WIP and nofralloc inline assembly fallback otherwise.
Inserted explicit dummy stack arrays under MUST_MATCH comments for unused stack space in ftCo_8009C5A4, ftCo_8009C540, and ftCo_8009C744-style routines.
Defined file-local MWERKS_GEKKO constants for 0.0, 1.0, and CONV_HI to replace the sdata2 constants removed from ft_08A4.s.
Preserved remaining ft_08A4.s assembly while deleting only the newly decompiled region, then added the new C objects ahead of the asm object in obj_files.mk.
Matched integer-from-float behavior in ftCo_8009C640 by expressing percent scaling with p_ftCommonData->x4A0/x4A4 and an int cast, backed by the inline-asm fallback for exact codegen.
Factored the ground/air shouldered timing expression into a small static inline helper inlineA0, returning x4A8 on ground and x4A8*x4AC in air.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match ftCo_Shouldered.c or remove its ASM fallback.
Search for further renames of mv.co.shouldered.x0 and mv.co.shouldered.x4.
Search for later HitCapsule layout refinements around damage, kb_angle, x8, and element.
Find subsequent PRs that continue decompiling asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s after ftCo_Shouldered.
Search for uses of victim_gobj to understand capture/throw ownership conventions.
